# S003. Stochastic Volatility Timing Model

## Positioning

This candidate uses a volatility state model as a timing overlay so the strategy can scale down before the pain arrives. It is most useful when the rest of the stack already has a signal and needs a risk-aware throttle.

## Reference Basis

- [The Heston stochastic volatility model with piecewise constant parameters - efficient calibration and pricing of window barrier options](../../references/papers/1805.04704_heston_piecewise_constant.pdf) - volatility regime calibration reference
- [Parameter Estimation of the Heston Volatility Model with Jumps in the Asset Prices](../../references/papers/2211.14814_heston_jumps.pdf) - jump-aware volatility reference

## Repo Anchors

### Operational Guardrails

Shared safety and execution rails used by every candidate before a signal can touch capital.
- [src/risk/manager.py](../../../src/risk/manager.py) - pre-trade risk gate and position haircut
- [src/brokers/paper.py](../../../src/brokers/paper.py) - paper-safe broker adapter
- [src/monitoring/health.py](../../../src/monitoring/health.py) - freshness, heartbeat, and kill-switch checks
- [src/backtest/engine.py](../../../src/backtest/engine.py) - deterministic offline backtest runner
- [src/backtest/matching_engine.py](../../../src/backtest/matching_engine.py) - queue-aware synthetic fill model
- [scripts/monitor_health.py](../../../scripts/monitor_health.py) - runtime health loop and stale-data monitor

### Market Data and Features

Point-in-time data loading plus the shared feature builder used by most signal stacks.
- [src/ingestion/loader.py](../../../src/ingestion/loader.py) - point-in-time market data loading
- [src/features/builder.py](../../../src/features/builder.py) - technical, lagged, and derived feature construction

### Regime and State

Regime detection and state overlays used when the signal should shrink or route rather than trade blind.
- [src/models/hmm.py](../../../src/models/hmm.py) - latent regime detector

## Data / Feature Bundle

- Use returns, realized volatility, and rolling drawdown features from the shared feature builder.
- Keep the volatility state aligned with the same freshness rules as the rest of the market data stack.
- Let the regime detector shrink exposure whenever the volatility posterior becomes unstable.

## Execution Path

- Forecast the next volatility regime and convert it into a position-size multiplier.
- Treat the result as a timing overlay instead of a directional alpha.
- Compare the overlay directly against the existing rolling-vol risk control in the backtest engine.

## Risk Controls

- Freeze the overlay when jumps or data gaps make the volatility estimate untrustworthy.
- Avoid letting a single bad volatility estimate trigger a large exposure jump.
- Do not use the model to lever up; it should only justify tighter or looser exposure.

## Validation Gates

- Check whether the overlay lowers max drawdown and tail loss on the same replay set used by smoke tests.
- Measure volatility forecast error versus a rolling historical baseline.
- Confirm the overlay improves risk-adjusted return after transaction costs.

## Archive Triggers

- Archive if the overlay does not improve drawdown or realized-vol accuracy.
- Archive if the state estimate is more brittle than the existing HMM risk gate.
- Archive if the signal is only useful in hindsight.

## Next Step

Use this as the volatility overlay reference for S011 and F041.
